words.exampleNL: Wat zijn je plannen voor de toekomst?
words.exampleEN: What are your plans for the future?

words.exampleNL: Mijn droom is om vloeiend Nederlands te spreken.
words.exampleEN: My dream is to speak fluent Dutch.
words.audioHint: Long 'oo' sound. One syllable. The 'dr' is like English.

words.exampleNL: Heb je al een plan voor het weekend?
words.exampleEN: Do you have a plan for the weekend yet?
words.audioHint: Identical to English pronunciation. Short 'a' sound.

words.exampleNL: Ik hoop dat het morgen mooi weer wordt.
words.exampleEN: I hope it will be nice weather tomorrow.
words.audioHint: Long 'o' sound. The verb form 'hoop' has double 'o'.

words.exampleNL: Ik wil volgend jaar naar Nederland verhuizen.
words.exampleEN: I want to move to the Netherlands next year.
words.audioHint: Short 'i' sound. An important modal verb in Dutch.
Short 'u' sound. Used to form future tense in Dutch.

words.exampleEN: I am going to study next year.
words.audioHint: Long 'aa' sound with guttural 'g'. Most common way to express future in Dutch.

words.exampleNL: Misschien ga ik morgen naar de markt.
words.exampleEN: Maybe I will go to the market tomorrow.
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable. The 'sch' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: Er komen veel veranderingen volgend jaar.
words.exampleEN: Many changes are coming next year.
words.audioHint: Four syllables. Stress on the second. From 'veranderen' (to change).

words.exampleNL: Mijn doel is om het inburgeringsexamen te halen.
words.exampleEN: My goal is to pass the civic integration exam.
words.audioHint: The 'oe' sounds like 'oo' in 'pool'. One syllable.

words.exampleNL: Dit is een goede kans om Nederlands te oefenen.
words.exampleEN: This is a good opportunity to practice Dutch.
words.audioHint: One syllable. Short 'a' sound. Same as English 'cans' but with a 'k'.

words.exampleNL: Ik beloof dat ik elke dag zal oefenen.
words.exampleEN: I promise that I will practice every day.
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable. Long 'o' sound in the middle.
